title: calamintha suaveolens subsp. langei
intro: Calamintha suaveolens subsp. langei, also known as sweet calamy, is a subspecies of the Calamintha suaveolens plant, which belongs to the mint family and is native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Calamintha suaveolens subsp. langei include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and can also be used as a garnish or added to salads for its culinary and medicinal value.
